item.title: Major Chatbots Found Unreliable on Elections, News Topics
item.description: Startup’s survey shows chatbots generally provide inaccurate answers when asked about news

item.title: Why It Might Take a Slowdown to Undo Hormuz Closure’s Economic Damage
item.description: As the Hormuz blockage nears the three-month mark, an inflation tipping point has been reached.

item.title: Chinese EV Maker BYD Extends Global Reach With a Small Shipping Fleet
item.description: Born of necessity during Covid, the vessels now help BYD control its supply chain amid geopolitical turmoil

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Rose to Almost Two-Month High in Latest Week
item.description: US mortgage rates increased last week to an almost two-month high, weighing on home purchase activity.

item.title: Chatbots Struggle With News Accuracy and Sourcing Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Four major artificial intelligence chatbots — OpenAI’s ChatGPT, Alphabet Inc.’s Google Gemini, Anthropic’s Claude and xAI’s Grok — are struggling to fairly and accurately answer questions about elections and geopolitics, according to a new study from Forum AI.

item.title: AI’s Promises Are Starting to Materialize – With a Human Cost
item.description: Markets are being built around it, powered by it, and priced because of it. But AI comes with a hefty human toll.

item.title: David Zahn Called the Gilt Rout. Now He’s Looking to Buy at 6%
item.description: David Zahn is looking vindicated. He’s been shunning UK bonds for more than a year, when other fund managers were piling in after selloffs.

item.title: China Cuts Spending the Most in Six Months in Blow to Economy
item.description: China scaled back government spending at its fastest pace in six months in April, a move that contributed to an unexpected slowdown in the economy across the board.

item.title: Modi’s Toffee Gift to Meloni Ignites Rally in Wrong Indian Stock
item.description: A packet of caramel toffees gifted by Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi to his Italian counterpart sent shares of a tiny software company soaring on Wednesday.

item.title: Tech Bulls Are Looking to Nvidia for Stocks Revival
item.description: Rising bond yields have been weighing on chip stocks.

item.title: France’s Moulin Backs Shared Euro Debt for New Projects
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France said he’s in favor of more joint debt issuance in the euro area for investment in the bloc’s new economic priorities.

item.title: UK House-Price Growth Grinds to a Halt After Loan Costs Surge
item.description: UK house prices failed to grow over the past year as headwinds from rising mortgage rates, political uncertainty and the Iran war build.

item.title: Tankmaker Rheinmetall Preps First Public Bond Deal Since 2010
item.description: Rheinmetall AG is preparing its first public bond sale in nearly 16 years as Europe’s defense industry booms.

item.title: Rice Prices Extend Rally to One-Year High as Crop Concerns Mount
item.description: A benchmark Asia rice price rose to the highest in more than a year, as worries loom over harvests across the region.

item.title: Russia and China Agree to Expand Key Cross-Border Rail Gateway
item.description: Russia and China signed an agreement on the joint construction of a new railway link across their shared border, part of a broader push to expand transport ties and trade between the two countries.

item.title: Indonesia’s Radical Export Experiment Upends Its Commodity Trade
item.description: Indonesia’s radical plan to take control of key commodity exports has left the country’s coal miners, palm-oil producers and traders racing to understand details of a policy that throws into upheaval one of the country’s biggest industries.

item.title: Intesa, ABN Amro Join Stablecoin Push to Blunt Dollar Dominance
item.description: A consortium of European banks seeking to launch a euro-denominated stablecoin has added 25 lenders to its ranks, speeding up efforts to build a viable alternative to dollar-denominated digital money.

item.title: Court Adviser Says Bolloré Controls Vivendi in Shareholder Spat
item.description: Billionaire Vincent Bolloré controlled Vivendi SE before a split of the media conglomerate in 2024, a Paris advocate general is set to say, a positive step for minority holders who want the billionaire to buy them out.

item.title: Tinder Is Betting Gen Z Daters Would Rather Be Offline
item.description: The dating app’s turnaround bets on live events, group dating and an AI-heavy redesign.

item.title: France’s Moulin Says Deficit Situation Serious, Not Catastrophic
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France said the country has a serious challenge to reduce its budget deficit, but the situation isn’t catastrophic.

item.title: Nvidia Earnings Are Set to Make or Break the Chip Stock Rally
item.description: For much of the year, chip stocks have been powering the market higher. Now, Nvidia Corp.’s earnings have a chance to confirm that the rally has more room to run — or add another brick to investors’ wall of worry.

item.title: Iran War Oil Disruption Prompts Nigeria Producers to Lift Output
item.description: Nigerian oil companies are plowing windfall gains from the Iran-war crude rally into near-term extraction projects, boosting the drive by Africa’s top producer to double output within four years.

item.title: ECB’s Wunsch Sees June Hike Quite Likely If War Doesn’t End
item.description: There’s a high probability that the European Central Bank will raise borrowing costs next month if the Iran war doesn’t end in the meantime, according to Governing Council member Pierre Wunsch.

item.title: Iceland Delivers Second Hike in Two Months to Cool Economy
item.description: Iceland’s central bank raised borrowing costs for a second time since the outbreak of the Middle East war to quell persistent price pressure.

item.title: China’s Palladium Imports Surge as Local Futures Lure Inflows
item.description: China imported a record amount of palladium last month, as local prices that have raced ahead of global ones lured inflows.

item.title: Billionaire Family’s Firm Taps Rothschild for Rare Qatar IPO
item.description: A Qatari conglomerate that’s part of the billionaire Al-Khayyat family’s empire is working with Rothschild & Co. on a potential initial public offering of its healthcare unit, according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be a rare listing on one of the Middle East’s quieter exchanges.

item.title: France’s Moulin Says ECB Must Be Ready to Act With Eye on Growth
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France said the European Central Bank must be ready to tighten monetary policy as inflation pressure rises, though he stressed that officials should also consider how their actions affect economic expansion.

item.title: Veteran UBS Analyst Says AI Is Helping End China Property Slump
item.description: UBS Group AG real estate analyst John Lam, a market veteran who spotted problems at China Evergrande Group before his peers, has joined a chorus of observers declaring that China’s yearslong property downturn is finally losing steam.

item.title: South African Inflation Hits 20-Month High on Iran War Shock
item.description: South African annual inflation accelerated to the highest level in 20 months in April, driven by sharp increases in domestic fuel costs stemming from the Iran war.

item.title: Cyprus Gets Back EU Finance Ministers Scared Off by Drone Attack
item.description: For Cyprus’s embattled tourist industry, this week’s arrival of 27 European Union finance ministers with an entourage of central bankers and officials is better late than never.
